codeReview.summary: The feature adds the requested Jest mock and a broad JS-surface contract suite, but the shipped default mock does not actually mirror the native module's missing-secret behavior: it reports `hasSecret=false` while `getSecret()` still resolves a secret. That contradiction can hide uninitialized-vault bugs in downstream Jest suites, so I am marking the review failed. The lifecycle test coverage is otherwise useful, but it currently works around the mock state instead of validating it directly.

issues.description: The default `NativeBiometricVault` mock is internally inconsistent: `getSecret()` resolves a hex secret even though `hasSecret()` defaults to `false` (`jest.setup.js:46-52`). Both native implementations reject `NOT_FOUND` when no alias exists (`android/.../NativeBiometricVaultModule.kt:302-305, 310-313`; `ios/.../RCTNativeBiometricVault.mm:95-96, 300-302`), so this mock can let downstream tests unlock an uninitialized vault and miss real bugs. The mock should either model an initialized state coherently (`hasSecret=true`) or reject `NOT_FOUND` until state has been provisioned.

The lifecycle roundtrip test scripts the state transition by manually flipping `mock.hasSecret.mockResolvedValueOnce(true)` instead of exercising state held by the shipped Jest mock. That means the test still passes even with the contradictory default mock above, so the claimed `generateAndStoreSecret -> hasSecret -> deleteSecret` coverage is weaker than advertised.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission docs disagree on the ninth canonical biometric error code. `AGENTS.md` still lists `TIMEOUT`, while the architecture doc and validation contract use `BIOMETRY_LOCKOUT_PERMANENT`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/home/liran/.factory/missions/08245e86-9e3a-49bb-ba11-c4ec0804b327/AGENTS.md:52` lists `..., VAULT_ERROR, TIMEOUT`; `/home/liran/src/enboxorg/mobile/.factory/library/architecture.md:141-146` and `/home/liran/.factory/missions/08245e86-9e3a-49bb-ba11-c4ec0804b327/validation-contract.md:572,1057-1064` list `BIOMETRY_LOCKOUT_PERMANENT` instead.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `mobile-feature-worker` skill says to write failing tests before implementation, but this worker edited `jest.setup.js` and created the test file before the first targeted test run. The handoff still reported `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`, so the deviation is not being surfaced.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/home/liran/src/enboxorg/mobile/.factory/skills/mobile-feature-worker/SKILL.md` section `Write failing tests first`; decoded transcript skeleton for session `d8cf0284-bf97-4556-8800-555e8d8c08d8` shows `Edit /home/liran/src/enboxorg/mobile/jest.setup.js` and `Create /home/liran/src/enboxorg/mobile/src/lib/enbox/__tests__/native-biometric-vault.test.ts` before the first `bun run test -- --testPathPattern native-biometric-vault` execution.

summary: Fail: the added Jest coverage is broad, but the shipped default `NativeBiometricVault` mock is semantically inconsistent with the real native modules because it reports no stored secret while still returning one from `getSecret()`. That can mask missing-initialization bugs in downstream tests, so the mock should be made coherent before accepting this feature.
